name: Devika
description: Open-source agentic AI software engineer from Stition.AI; takes a high-level objective and runs a multi-agent loop (planning, research, coding, execution, reporting) through a local Flask + Svelte web UI.

description.value: Open-source agentic AI software engineer that breaks down a high-level objective into steps, researches the web, generates code, and reports results via a local Flask + Svelte web UI. Initially positioned as an OSS alternative to Cognition's Devin.

website.comment: Repo README states explicitly: "DEVIKA DOES NOT HAVE AN OFFICIAL WEBSITE". Several look-alike sites exist (devikaai.org, devikaai.co) but are unaffiliated.

creator.comment: Created by Mufeed VH, founder of Stition.AI.

first-release.comment: Project went public in March 2024 (around 2024-03-21) following Devin's announcement.

latest-release.comment: Repo has no tagged releases ("There aren't any releases here"). Most recent commit on main was 2025-09-25 (a README update); the project README points users to a successor called "Opcode".

pricing-model.comment: OSS only; no hosted SaaS or paid tier. Users supply their own LLM API keys.

covered-phases.comment: Documented loop: prompt → Planner step list → Researcher web browsing (discovery) → Coder → Runner → Reporter. No distinct spec, architecture, review, testing, deployment, or retrospective phase.

methodology-style.comment: Architecture defines distinct sub-agent roles (Planner, Researcher, Coder, Action, Runner, Feature, Patcher, Reporter, Decision) orchestrated by an Agent Core; the value is in the runtime/harness rather than a prescriptive spec methodology.

opinionated.comment: Workflow is fixed in code but not enforced as a methodology the user must follow; the user's interaction is just a chat objective with iterative refinement.

ceremony-level.comment: No ADRs, specs, or formal review gates; the user issues an objective and reviews the result.

mechanization-level.comment: After setup, a single `python devika.py` plus the Svelte UI runs the entire planning-to-coding loop end-to-end without per-step CLI invocation.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: No formal per-phase approval gate; the user can chat to redirect the agent but the loop does not pause for explicit approvals between Planner / Researcher / Coder.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Default loop runs prompt → plan → research → code → execution → report autonomously.

autonomy-level.comment: Default config runs the full loop autonomously; user intervention is via chat, not enforced approval gates. Not 5 because the user is expected to iterate via the chat UI.

rollback-support.comment: No first-class undo/rollback for generated artifacts; reverts rely on git in the user's project directory.

supported-agents.comment: Devika ships its own agent runtime; it is not a workflow layered on Claude Code, Cursor, etc. Underlying LLMs are pluggable (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, Mistral, Groq, Ollama).

editor-integrations.comment: Local Svelte web UI on http://127.0.0.1:3001; no VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, or pure CLI mode.

model-agnostic.comment: Pluggable LLM layer supports Claude 3, GPT-4, Gemini, Mistral, Groq, and local models via Ollama / LM Studio.

mcp-support.comment: No MCP integration documented in README, ARCHITECTURE.md, or repo.

artifact-formats.comment: Primary outputs are source code files; the Reporter can produce a PDF report and conversation history is stored in SQLite. No structured markdown spec/plan artifacts are produced for git.

artifacts-covered.comment: Planner generates step-by-step plans (held in agent state, not committed); Reporter generates project documentation (PDF). No specs, ADRs, roadmap, change logs, test plans, or release notes.

project-layout.value: Fixed Devika repo layout: `db/` (SQLite), `data/` and `projects/` (generated code per project), `config.toml` (auto-generated, holds API keys and endpoints), `src/agents/` (sub-agent prompt templates in Jinja). No convention is imposed on the user's own project tree.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Plans, agent state, and conversation history live in a local SQLite database under `db/`, not in the user's git repo. Generated code can of course be checked in, but workflow artifacts are not.

language-agnostic.comment: The Coder agent is described as multi-language; no fixed target stack is enforced.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Process discipline is encoded in agent prompts only; no hooks, linters, test gates, agent-review, CI checks, or manual-approval gates ship as part of the workflow.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Agent loop is internally sequenced but there is no mechanism that refuses ad-hoc user re-prompting or out-of-order interaction.

artifact-validation.comment: No schema or structural validation of agent outputs; plans and reports are free-form.

memory-implementation.comment: Persistent state stored in a local SQLite database via SQLModel: `Projects` table (per-project conversation history as JSON) and `AgentStateModel` table (per-project agent thoughts, interactions, token usage).

persistent-memory.comment: Project state and conversation history persist across sessions in the local SQLite DB.

project-context-files.comment: No CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md style project-context file is consumed or produced; configuration lives in `config.toml`.

cost-tracking.comment: Per-conversation token usage is tracked in `AgentStateModel` and exposed via the `/api/token-usage` and `/api/calculate-tokens` endpoints. No budget-limit halt feature.

run-history.comment: Conversations and agent state sequences are recorded per project in SQLite; the UI also surfaces browser snapshots.

telemetry-opt.comment: No explicit telemetry policy or opt-in/opt-out toggle found in the README, ARCHITECTURE.md, or config; the project runs locally and there is no obvious telemetry collection, but this is not stated.

custom-templates.comment: Sub-agent prompt templates (Jinja) live in the repo and could be edited by forking, but no first-class user-facing template customization API is documented.

sub-agents.comment: Internal sub-agents (Planner, Researcher, Coder, etc.) are hardcoded in the architecture; users cannot define their own personas/roles/skills via configuration.

marketplace.comment: No marketplace, plugin registry, or shared template repository.

install-mechanism.comment: Install path is `git clone` + Python venv (3.10–3.12) + `uv pip install -r requirements.txt` + Playwright browsers + `bun install` for the Svelte UI. No published package on PyPI/npm.

setup-time.comment: Multiple prerequisites (Python 3.10–3.12, uv, Playwright with browser download, bun, Node, plus configuring API keys for at least one LLM and a search provider) push first-run setup past 30 minutes for most users.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine; no hosted version exists.

requires-account.comment: No vendor account; users supply their own LLM and search-provider API keys via `config.toml`.

git-worktree-support.comment: No documented use of or support for git worktrees; project isolation is per-folder under `projects/`.

documentation-quality.comment: README + ARCHITECTURE.md cover the core workflow and install steps but the project self-describes as "very early development/experimental stage"; no separate docs site, API reference, or how-to guides.

github-stars.comment: 19.5k stars as displayed on the repo on 2026-04-21.

active-maintenance.comment: Most recent commit on main is 2025-09-25 (a README update pointing users to a successor project, "Opcode"); previous code commits date to August 2024. Outside the 90-day window from 2026-04-21.

example-projects.comment: No official example repos or documented case studies in the project; third-party blog posts exist but are not first-party.

community-channel.comment: Repo links a Discord server and has GitHub Discussions enabled.
